divergence: Electricity was declared a controlled substance and is sold by apothecaries
story_angle: breathless coverage of a trivial event treated as the hinge of history
headline: Dustspire Enclave Seizes Archaeology of Blame for 23 Lives Lost to Electromagnetic Famine
deck: Apothecary's Volt-Pouch Delay Dubbed 'A Curious Incident of Military Absence'
article:
Dustspire enclave pinned the perimeter breach and 23 radstorm deaths on Crankley’s Apothecary and Supply after the convoy arrived late. Regulators traced the holdup to outdated brass-caravan rules that the apothecary still treated as iron law.

Lights failed forty-seven minutes after the last stored current ran out. Survivor Bartholomew Nox recalled trading scavenged casings for a black-market jolt, then watching the settlement go dark. District Overseer Helga Voltsworth defended the rationing rules, insisting that electrons cannot be spent without paperwork.

Survivors now log the losses in the margins of their ration books, each entry another reminder that one late volt-pouch can empty an entire district.
author: Gideon B. Rust, Chief Scribe of Controlled Currents

weather.condition: Sunny with a chance of regret

oped.title: Hats Off to Electromagnetic Prudence: The Enclave's Seizure Saves Us From Bare-Headed Folly
oped.author: Professor Reginald P. Blunderbuss, Chair of Misapplied Voltage Ethics at the Wasteland Academy of Selective Memory
oped.body: Dustspire deserves credit for locking away the Archaeology of Blame before it could smear the apothecaries who ration current by the book. The twenty-three who died simply lacked copper-threaded hats; proper millinery would have conducted the famine away. The so-called military absence is a mercy that keeps jackboots out of private volt commerce. My cousin’s shop keeps flawless records and would never have delayed had the deceased arrived wearing hats fit for his shelves. Families ought to apologize for their headwear instead of demanding scarce power. In this wasteland, style still decides who keeps the lights on.

classifieds.text: Fresh Archaeology of Blame shards from the Dustspire raid; ideal for shifting electromagnetic famine blame onto any militia that skipped town. Trading for three live rad-goats before auditors reach the cellar.

Two scavengers needed to follow the trail of the missing military escort tied to the twenty-three deaths; must avoid apothecary patrols and return any stray volt-vials. Payment in unmarked blame tokens and hazard rations.
Crankley’s Apothecary admits the volt-pouch delay was a calculated retreat from Dustspire inspectors; regrets to the twenty-three households now counting hours by dark.
One full volt-pouch and the Enclave’s final excuse for the famine, last seen at the apothecary’s rear entrance at dusk, 2263. Return the blame; keep the pouch.

panels.caption: 'Volt-pouch is late again,' the apothecary mutters as 23 lights die in Dustspire.
Enclave captain seizes the scroll: 'This Archaeology of Blame proves the convoy never came.'
'Case closed,' he stamps. 'Next famine we blame the scribes who wrote it.'

joke.setup: Dustspire's council announced they had seized the Archaeology of Blame to explain the 23 dead from the latest electromagnetic famine.
joke.punchline: Turns out the real shortage wasn't volts—it was anyone left who hadn't already been blamed for the last five famines.
joke.text: Dustspire's council announced they had seized the Archaeology of Blame to explain the 23 dead from the latest electromagnetic famine. Turns out the real shortage wasn't volts—it was anyone left who hadn't already been blamed for the last five famines.

sponsor_ads.headline: Archaeology of Blame: Apothecary Extracts
sponsor_ads.body: Dustspire fragments now bottled as blame-redirection tincture; one drop moves electromagnetic famine from your hovel to the next scavenger down the line.
sponsor_ads.tagline: 23 prior users reported mild historical nausea.

sponsor_ads.headline: Controlled Current: Famine Insurance Ampoules
sponsor_ads.body: Measured volts that won’t add to any twenty-three-body count; Enclave seal ensures your dose stays unblamed.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Electricity possession requires current blame chit.

sponsor_ads.headline: Dustspire Public Notice: Blame Redistribution Service
sponsor_ads.body: Trade unspent electromagnetic guilt for credit toward next week’s rations; famine deaths clear faster when the Archaeology points elsewhere.

sponsor_ads.headline: Static Veil Charms – Wasteland Edition
sponsor_ads.body: Carved from yesterday’s seized ruins, these charms absorb the first twenty-three volts of blame before the lights fail permanently.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Apothecary prescription mandatory; side effects include sudden enclave loyalty.

stories.headline: Enclave Auditors Seize Ledger of Missing Volt Pouches Linked to Famine
stories.deck: Courier Admits Pouch Was 'Left Beside a Radiator That No Longer Works'
stories.article:
Auditors traced three missing volt pouches to a courier route erased by sandstorm. The apothecary guild now demands the guard corps repay the charges counted among the famine dead.

Seventeen signatures in the ledger were inked with charcoal from spent battery casings. The courier has been reassigned to crank detail while the blame ledger grows.

stories.headline: Irrigation Rigs Stand Idle as Volt Allotments Diverted to Archive Vaults
stories.deck: Cooperative Reports Loss of 400 Hectares to Static-Induced Blight
stories.article:
Outer-terrace growers asked for emergency crank permits after their volt allotment was rerouted to power the Archaeology of Blame excavation. Pumps stopped, salt crept across millet fields, and yields fell another nine percent.

Guild ledgers list the diversion as an administrative priority rather than a controlled-substance failure.

real_world_echo: obliquely mirrors Ukraine interceptor missile shortage warning amid Kyiv deaths by recasting electricity rationing as the cause of raid fatalities
